rule_name: Privilege Escalation — Admin Policy Attached to User
description: Detects when AdministratorAccess, PowerUserAccess, or IAMFullAccess managed policies are attached to an IAM user. This is the most direct form of privilege escalation in AWS — an attacker with iam:AttachUserPolicy permission can grant themselves full admin access in a single API call.
query_kql: (eventName:"AttachUserPolicy" OR eventName:"AttachRolePolicy" OR eventName:"PutUserPolicy") AND (request_policy_arn:*AdministratorAccess* OR request_policy_arn:*PowerUserAccess* OR request_policy_arn:*IAMFullAccess*)
triage_steps: Identify WHO received the policy (requestParameters.userName or requestParameters.roleName)
Determine if this was an approved change (check change management tickets)
Review subsequent actions by the user who received the elevated permissions
Check if the attaching principal's credentials may be compromised
false_positive_notes: Legitimate scenarios: new admin onboarding, emergency break-glass procedures, terraform/CloudFormation deployments. Cross-reference with IaC pipelines and change management.
tuning_guidance: Exclude known IaC service roles (e.g., terraform-deployer) and CI/CD pipelines. Consider broadening to detect any policy attachment, not just admin-level, for higher-security environments.
response_actions: Investigate the principal who performed the attachment
Review all actions taken by the target user/role after the policy was attached
If unauthorized, disable both the attaching and receiving principals
Audit all IAM policy attachments in the last 24 hours for similar patterns
